title: Workplace Violence Prevention Training Quiz Answers
source_filename: Workplace Violence Prevention Training Quiz Answers.docx

qas.q: Of the four types of violence, which is most common in healthcare settings?
Which federal statute requires all employers to provide a work environment "free from recognized hazards that are causing or are likely to cause death or serious physical harm?"
qas.a: gGeneral Duty Clause of the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970

qas.q: Workplace health and safety education programs should be directed toward which employees?
qas.a: All employees including supervisors, managers and senior staff

qas.q: Which of these is typically not a behavior that presages violence?

Per OSHA guidelines, the five major components of an effective safety and health program are: management commitment & hazard prevention and control & safety and health training & recordkeeping and program evaluation, & ...
Basic measures individual nurses can take to minimize risk of workplace violence include all of the following except:
qas.a: Working as many double shifts as allowed to gain experience

qas.q: Which of these is not an example of non-verbal intervention?
Organizations should do which of the following for employees after a violent incident?
qas.a: Provide non-mandatory access to counselors for all traumatized workers

qas.q: There is no need to file a written incident report when?
qas.a: The patient seemed agitated but did not act out physically or verbally

qas.q: Successful workplace violence prevention programs employ reporting procedures that...
qas.a: Empower nurses to come forward when incidents occur
